examples.title: Pseudocode: Minimum Distance Classifier
examples.description: Classify an unknown pattern using the Minimum Distance Classifier
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MinimumDistanceClassifier
INPUT: unknown pattern vector x, class prototypes μ₁, μ₂, ..., μ_c
OUTPUT: assigned class label

BEGIN
 FOR EACH class prototype μ_i
 calculate Euclidean distance d(x, μ_i)
 END FOR
 assign x to class j with minimum distance d(x, μ_j)
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the Minimum Distance Classifier algorithm. It iterates over each class prototype, calculates the Euclidean distance between the unknown pattern and the prototype, and assigns the pattern to the class with the minimum distance.

examples.title: Python: Minimum Distance Classifier
examples.description: Implement the Minimum Distance Classifier in Python
examples.code:
import numpy as np

def minimum_distance_classifier(x, prototypes):
 distances = [np.linalg.norm(x - prototype) for prototype in prototypes]
 return np.argmin(distances)

# Example usage:
prototypes = np.array([[1.67, 2.00], [4.67, 4.67]])
x = np.array([4, 3])
assigned_class = minimum_distance_classifier(x, prototypes)
print(assigned_class)
examples.explanation: This Python code implements the Minimum Distance Classifier using NumPy. It calculates the Euclidean distance between the unknown pattern and each class prototype, and returns the index of the prototype with the minimum distance.

examples.title: C: Minimum Distance Classifier
examples.description: Implement the Minimum Distance Classifier in C
examples.explanation: This C code implements the Minimum Distance Classifier using a separate function for calculating the Euclidean distance. It iterates over each class prototype, calculates the distance, and returns the index of the prototype with the minimum distance.
